candidate.name: a2-no-steal-sleep
candidate.hypothesis: The high clock_nanosleep count observed in ylong_base HTTPS proxy benchmarks may come from ylong_runtime::executor::MultiThreadScheduler::get_task_from_searching sleeping for 1 microsecond between failed steal attempts. Removing that fixed sleep could reduce scheduler/timer overhead without changing HTTP proxy, CONNECT, TLS, mTLS, or benchmark baseline semantics.
candidate.root_cause_signal: strace-diagnostic shows ylong_http_client and libcurl have similar recvfrom/sendto counts, while ylong has much higher clock_nanosleep/futex/epoll_wait/sched_yield activity. Source review found thread::sleep(Duration::from_micros(1)) in the ylong_runtime steal-search path.
temporary_changes: Temporarily patched workspace Cargo.toml to resolve ylong_runtime, ylong_io, and ylong_runtime_macros from the vendored runtime path.
Temporarily removed thread::sleep(Duration::from_micros(1)) between get_task_from_searching steal attempts, leaving the same three steal attempts.
Applied the Rust 1.96 join_set.rs dangerous_implicit_autorefs compatibility fix required to compile the vendored runtime.

commands.phase_timing_benchmark: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --bench-bin /tmp/ylong-http-a2-nostealsleep-target/release/https_proxy_bench --scenario https-over-https-proxy --requests 200,1000 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --concurrency 8 --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --baseline libcurl --phase-timing --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/a2-no-steal-sleep-candidate --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/a2-no-steal-sleep-candidate
commands.nonphase_benchmark: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --bench-bin /tmp/ylong-http-a2-nostealsleep-target/release/https_proxy_bench --scenario https-over-https-proxy --requests 200,1000 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --concurrency 8 --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --baseline libcurl --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/a2-no-steal-sleep-nonphase --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/a2-no-steal-sleep-nonphase
commands.postrollback_cargo_tree: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_NET_OFFLINE=true CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong-http-a2-nostealsleep-postrollback-target cargo tree --offline -p ylong_http_client --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0,libcurl_bench -i ylong_runtime
commands.postrollback_cargo_test: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_NET_OFFLINE=true CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong-http-a2-nostealsleep-postrollback-target cargo test --offline -p ylong_http_client --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0,libcurl_bench --bin https_proxy_bench -- --nocapture

decision.reason: The candidate improves the small 200-request phase-timing row but fails S9/SOTA gates in both focused matrices. The 1000-request row has lower throughput than libcurl and worse p50, p95, CPU/request, and RSS. Removing the steal-search micro-sleep is therefore counterexample evidence only and must not be retained as a production or report claim.
